CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Continued To Jun-12-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Text Ruling

Plaintiff Scott Ayers, Ilona Marsovszki Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses (Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Off Calendar

Motion To Strike/Tax Costs; Decl. Of Jimmy Y. Park; Exhibit A
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jimmy Park, Esq. of Bowman & Brooke LLP, T:310.768.3068, for defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
Deepak Devabose, Esq. of Knight Law Group, T:310.552.2250, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs is granted in part. $960 of the $4916.37 in claimed costs are taxed, resulting in $3956.37 in costs recoverable by plaintiffs Scott Ayers and Ilona Marsovszki against Hyundai. Hyundai's argument that the recoverability of plaintiffs' claimed costs should be determined by the standards in the general costs statute (CCP 1033.5) rather than the costs provision of the Song-Beverly Act (Civil Code 1794(d)) because plaintiffs filed a costs memorandum lacks merit. No case holds that by filing a costs memorandum a prevailing Song-Beverly plaintiff forfeits or waives his right to seek costs under the more generous standard in section 1794(d). Hyundai's argument puts form over substance and is contrary to the remedial purposes of the Song-Beverly Act. Thus, the portions of Hyundai's motion seeking to strike or tax costs based on non-recoverability of those costs per section 1033.5(b) are denied. $830 for claimed service of process costs is taxed because plaintiffs have not shown that the costs for serving the seven deposition subpoenas were reasonably incurred given that the depositions never occurred. Plaintiffs have shown that the expert fees of Thomas Lepper were reasonably incurred. $130 for claimed appearance attorneys is taxed because those appearances were only necessitated by plaintiffs' delay in filing their fees motion. Plaintiffs have shown that all other claimed "other" expenses - attorney services and messengers for court filings and service, travel, postage, and copies - were reasonably incurred.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, email: mtorreano@sftc.org; torreanom@aol.com
